title: Enemy Targeting
summary: Give the enemy a clear detection range and chase behavior.
playerOutcome: When the player moves close enough, the enemy visibly turns and chases them; outside that range it remains idle.
whyItMatters: The mechanic makes the sample world visibly react to the player.
baselineBehavior: The player moves, while the visible enemy remains idle at every distance.
expectedBehavior: The enemy stays idle outside 220 pixels, detects and chases the player inside 220 pixels, and stops again when the player retreats.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The enemy remains idle while the player is outside the detection range.
The enemy reports detection while the player is inside the detection range.
The enemy moves toward the detected player.
The Godot project passes the pinned headless verification command.
A user can launch the game and visibly confirm idle, detection, and chase behavior.

verification.instruction: Run npm run demo:play, move into and out of range, and observe the enemy switch between IDLE and CHASING.
